rereviews.candidate_source_assessment: Accepted candidate sources support that OpenClaw documents browser-based Control UI/WebChat, mobile-related WebChat or node behavior, and Hermes migration context. They do not directly establish that OpenClaw is a web or phone interface specifically for using Hermes Agent.
rereviews.caveat_text: Use only with a caveat that the cited sources document OpenClaw web/mobile-facing interfaces and Hermes migration context, not a confirmed Hermes Agent-specific web or phone interface.
rereviews.combined_evidence_assessment: Combined evidence partially supports a narrower statement that OpenClaw has web and mobile-related interfaces and has Hermes migration/import context. It does not fully support the broader claim that these interfaces are for using Hermes Agent specifically.
rereviews.contradiction_notes: No direct contradiction found in the accepted candidate summaries, but the Hermes Agent-specific interface claim is not directly proven.
rereviews.limitations: Candidate sources are limited to OpenClaw documentation summaries. They do not include code-level verification, release notes, configuration evidence, or direct confirmation that Hermes Agent itself is operated through these interfaces.
rereviews.original_source_assessment: The original source was assessed as unsupported. That remains appropriate for the Hermes Agent-specific portion of the statement if relying on the original source alone.
rereviews.original_statement: The project metadata suggests a web or phone interface for using Hermes Agent.
rereviews.residual_risk: Moderate risk of overstatement if phrased as a Hermes Agent interface rather than an OpenClaw interface with Hermes migration context.
rereviews.why_not_author_approved: Run 15 is advisory-only, and the evidence only partially supports a narrowed, caveated review note; it does not authorize claim insertion, authoring, or publication.

rereviews.candidate_source_assessment: Accepted candidate sources support that OpenClaw documentation names Hermes in migration/setup contexts, including a bundled Hermes migration provider and onboarding/import behavior when Hermes state is detected. They do not establish Hermes as a runtime dependency or full operating environment.
rereviews.caveat_text: Frame narrowly: OpenClaw documentation names Hermes in migration/setup tooling contexts; do not state that Hermes is a runtime dependency or general operating environment without additional sources.
rereviews.combined_evidence_assessment: Combined evidence partially corroborates the weak agent-adjacent tooling claim: OpenClaw and Hermes are connected through migration/import tooling, but the evidence should be framed as migration or setup context rather than broad environment support.
rereviews.contradiction_notes: No contradiction found in the accepted candidate summaries.
rereviews.limitations: Sources are documentation summaries for migration and setup only. They do not prove execution-environment status, dependency requirements, or general Hermes Agent runtime integration.
rereviews.original_source_assessment: The original source was assessed as partially supported. That remains reasonable because the original statement is weakly phrased but still risks implying more than the available source proves.
rereviews.original_statement: The metadata weakly shows agent-adjacent tooling that names OpenClaw and Hermes as relevant environments.
rereviews.residual_risk: Low to moderate risk if preserved as a narrow review note; higher risk if expanded into a broad environment or dependency claim.
rereviews.why_not_author_approved: Run 15 is advisory-only. Partial corroboration may support review-note persistence, but it does not permit authoring, claim insertion, or publication.

accepted_candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Direct public project documentation for OpenClaw interface/channel claims, including Web Control UI, WebChat, mobile nodes, and gateway architecture.
accepted_candidate_sources.limitations: This page supports OpenClaw web/mobile access generally; it does not by itself prove a Hermes Agent-specific interface.
accepted_candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The docs describe OpenClaw as a gateway across chat apps, WebChat, mobile nodes, and a Web Control UI, which can corroborate the web/phone interface part of the item.
accepted_candidate_sources.safe_summary: OpenClaw docs describe a self-hosted multi-channel gateway for AI agents with WebChat, mobile nodes, a Web Control UI, and phone-reachable messaging channels.
accepted_candidate_sources.title: OpenClaw documentation home

accepted_candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Direct public documentation for the browser-based OpenClaw Control UI.
accepted_candidate_sources.limitations: Supports the web-interface portion only; it does not prove phone/mobile or Hermes-specific claims.
accepted_candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The item asks whether OpenClaw documentation supports a web interface; this page documents the browser Control UI and its local URLs/auth behavior.
accepted_candidate_sources.safe_summary: The Control UI is documented as a browser-based Gateway control UI for chat, activity, nodes, config, logs, updates, and related operations.
accepted_candidate_sources.title: Control UI

accepted_candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Direct public documentation for WebChat and macOS/iOS chat UI behavior.
accepted_candidate_sources.limitations: The page supports OpenClaw WebChat/mobile UI behavior but not necessarily Hermes Agent-specific use.
accepted_candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The item asks about phone/mobile interface support; this page documents WebChat and notes macOS/iOS SwiftUI chat UI communicating with the Gateway WebSocket.
accepted_candidate_sources.safe_summary: OpenClaw WebChat docs describe a native macOS/iOS chat UI and Control UI chat tab using the Gateway WebSocket and shared sessions/routing.
accepted_candidate_sources.title: WebChat

accepted_candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Direct public documentation for iOS/Android/headless nodes and device capabilities.
accepted_candidate_sources.limitations: Nodes are peripherals rather than the primary user chat interface; this is context rather than direct proof of the original broad statement.
accepted_candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The item asks about mobile/phone-related interfaces; node docs give public context for iOS/Android companion nodes and Gateway pairing.
accepted_candidate_sources.safe_summary: OpenClaw node docs describe companion devices including macOS, iOS, Android, and headless nodes that connect to the Gateway WebSocket and expose capabilities.
accepted_candidate_sources.title: Nodes

accepted_candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Public OpenClaw documentation mentioning Hermes in a migration context.
accepted_candidate_sources.limitations: Does not prove OpenClaw is a web/phone interface for Hermes Agent; it only documents migration from Hermes to OpenClaw.
accepted_candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: This candidate helps bound the Hermes-specific part of the item by showing an official OpenClaw/Hermes relationship, but only as migration support.
accepted_candidate_sources.safe_summary: OpenClaw migration docs describe a bundled Hermes migration provider that previews and imports selected Hermes state into OpenClaw.
accepted_candidate_sources.title: Migrating from Hermes to OpenClaw

candidate_source_limitations: This page supports OpenClaw web/mobile access generally; it does not by itself prove a Hermes Agent-specific interface.
Supports the web-interface portion only; it does not prove phone/mobile or Hermes-specific claims.

selected_items.original_statement: The project metadata suggests a web or phone interface for using Hermes Agent.
selected_items.what_needs_corroboration: Whether the OpenClaw project documentation, code, release notes, or configuration explicitly supports a web interface, phone/mobile interface, and Hermes Agent-specific environment or dependency claim.

accepted_candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Official CLI documentation for migration providers, including Hermes.
accepted_candidate_sources.limitations: Supports Hermes as a migration source/integration context, not necessarily as an execution environment or runtime dependency.
accepted_candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The item asks whether OpenClaw docs/code explicitly name Hermes as a relevant environment, dependency, integration target, or adjacent operating context; this page explicitly documents the Hermes migration provider and commands.
accepted_candidate_sources.safe_summary: The OpenClaw migrate CLI docs list Hermes as a bundled migration provider and document dry-run/apply commands for importing Hermes state.
accepted_candidate_sources.title: openclaw migrate

accepted_candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Official setup CLI documentation that references Hermes import during onboarding/setup.
accepted_candidate_sources.limitations: This is a setup/import option reference and does not establish a broad Hermes operating environment claim.
accepted_candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The setup docs mention --import-from hermes and --import-source ~/.hermes, which corroborates a Hermes-adjacent onboarding path.
accepted_candidate_sources.safe_summary: The setup docs include examples for onboarding/import from Hermes and note that interactive onboarding can offer migration when Hermes state is detected.
accepted_candidate_sources.title: openclaw setup

candidate_source_limitations: Supports Hermes as a migration source/integration context, not necessarily as an execution environment or runtime dependency.
This is a setup/import option reference and does not establish a broad Hermes operating environment claim.
